title: 10 best herbal teas for heartburn
intro: Herbal teas, especially those made from chamomile, licorice root, marshmallow root, and slippery elm, can help alleviate heartburn symptoms. These herbs have anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe the irritated esophageal lining caused by acid reflux. They can also help reduce gastric acid production, making the stomach environment less acidic. Additionally, some herbs, like licorice root, create a protective coating on the esophageal and stomach lining, preventing acid from causing further irritation. Furthermore, these teas can stimulate the production of mucus in the gastrointestinal tract, providing a barrier against stomach acid. Lastly, herbal teas can promote digestion and relaxation, reducing stress-induced heartburn.

remedies.remedy_desc: Chamomile tea helps with heartburn because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, which can cause heartburn. It contains antispasmodic properties that help relax the muscles of the digestive tract, preventing acid reflux and easing heartburn symptoms. The tea also has a calming effect on the body, which can help reduce stress and anxiety that can trigger heartburn. Furthermore, chamomile tea has been shown to increase the production of mucus in the stomach, forming a protective barrier against stomach acid. Lastly, its soothing and gentle nature makes it a safer alternative to over-the-counter heartburn medications, which can have side effects such as stomach ulcers or kidney damage.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Chamomile's daisy-like flowers are used for medicinal tea for heartburn due to their anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help soothe irritation in the esophagus.
Leaves: The small, feathery leaves of chamomile contain soothing compounds, making them a gentle choice for medicinal tea to alleviate heartburn symptoms.
Seeds: Chamomile seeds are rich in calming oils and constituents, which can help relax the digestive muscles and reduce the discomfort of heartburn when consumed as a tea.
remedy_constituents: Azulene: Azulene has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce irritation and discomfort in the digestive tract, alleviating heartburn symptoms.
Bisabolol: This compound has anti-spasmodic properties, which can help relax the muscles in the digestive tract and prevent acid reflux.
Matricin: Matricin is a precursor to chamazulene, which has anti-inflammatory effects and can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus caused by heartburn.
Apigenin: Apigenin has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help protect the lining of the stomach and esophagus from damage, preventing heartburn symptoms.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids in chamomile have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help reduce inflammation and prevent muscle spasms in the digestive tract, alleviating heartburn.
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water in a kettle or pot for 3-5 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried chamomile flowers to a tea infuser or teapot.
Pour the hot water over the chamomile flowers and let steep for 5-7 minutes.
Remove the tea infuser or strain the tea into a cup.
Enjoy your soothing chamomile tea, which may help alleviate heartburn symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tea helps with heartburn because it contains a substance called mucilage, which becomes slippery and thick when mixed with water, forming a protective coating on the esophageal lining, preventing stomach acid from causing irritation. This soothing effect reduces inflammation and pain, making it an effective natural remedy for heartburn. Moreover, marshmallow tea has anti-inflammatory properties that further help in healing the damaged tissue in the esophagus. Additionally, it can increase the mucus secretion in the gastrointestinal tract, providing further protection against stomach acid. Marshmallow tea is a caffeine-free and gentle herbal tea, making it a safer alternative to antacids, which can have side effects with long-term use.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Marshmallow leaves are often used in medicinal tea for heartburn due to their high mucilage content, which can help protect and soothe the digestive tract.
Flowers: The flowers of the marshmallow plant contain anti-inflammatory properties, making them useful in tea for heartburn relief.
Rhyzomes: Marshmallow rhyzomes have demulcent properties, which means they can help alleviate irritation and inflammation in the digestive system.
Seeds: Marshmallow seeds contain mucilage, which can help form a protective layer on the mucous membranes of the digestive tract and provide relief from heartburn.
remedy_constituents: Mucilage: The mucilage in marshmallow forms a slick gel when mixed with water, which coats and soothes the esophagus and stomach lining, reducing irritation and inflammation caused by acid reflux.

Polysaccharides: These complex carbohydrates form a protective layer on the mucous membranes, helping to prevent stomach acid from coming into contact with and damaging the esophagus, thereby alleviating heartburn symptoms.

Flavonoids: Certain flavonoids found in marshmallow, like rutin and quercetin, have anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and providing relief from heartburn.

Ascorbic Acid: Marshmallow contains small amounts of vitamin C, which supports overall immune function and aids in wound healing, helping the esophagus and stomach lining recover from damage caused by acid reflux.
Phenolic Acids: These compounds have antioxidant properties that can help protect the stomach lining from oxidative stress and damage, reducing the severity of heartburn symptoms.
remedy_recipe: In a small pot, pour 1 cup of water and bring it to a simmer.
Add 1 tablespoon of dried marshmallow root, reduce heat to low, and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Remove pot from heat, strain the tea into a cup, and discard the marshmallow root.
Drink tea while warm, up to three times a day for heartburn relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Slippery elm tea helps with heartburn because it contains a substance called mucilage, which becomes slippery and thick when mixed with water. This slippery substance coats the lining of the esophagus, providing a protective barrier against stomach acid. As a result, slippery elm tea can help reduce the irritation and inflammation caused by acid reflux, providing relief from heartburn symptoms. Additionally, slippery elm tea can help increase the production of saliva, which can neutralize stomach acid and help wash it down the esophagus. Slippery elm tea is also thought to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may further help soothe and heal the esophageal lining. Overall, slippery elm tea can provide a natural and effective way to manage heartburn and promote digestive health.
remedy_parts: Bark: The inner bark of the slippery elm tree is used to make medicinal tea for heartburn, as it contains a substance called mucilage that can help protect and soothe the digestive tract.
Leaves: Slippery elm leaves are used in teas for heartburn due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce irritation in the esophagus.
Seeds: Slippery elm seeds are utilized in teas for heartburn as they are a source of mucilage, which can help create a protective barrier in the stomach and reduce acidity.

Buds: The buds of the slippery elm tree are included in teas for heartburn because they contain high amounts of mucilage, which can help alleviate symptoms of heartburn by coating and soothing the digestive tract.
remedy_constituents: Mucilage: Slippery elm's high mucilage content forms a slick gel when mixed with water, which coats and soothes irritated mucous membranes, helping to alleviate heartburn by protecting the esophageal lining from stomach acid.

Tannins: These astringent compounds found in slippery elm tighten and constrict tissues, reducing inflammation and providing relief from heartburn by helping to prevent stomach acid from backing up into the esophagus.

Starch: Slippery elm contains a significant amount of starch, which, when combined with mucilage, forms a protective barrier over the mucous membranes, providing soothing relief for heartburn symptoms.
Gallic acid: This polyphenol antioxidant, present in slippery elm, helps reduce inflammation, healing the damaged esophageal lining and alleviating heartburn discomfort.

Kaempferol: A flavonoid found in slippery elm, kaempferol has anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antispasmodic properties that can help ease heartburn symptoms by reducing inflammation, scavenging free radicals, and relaxing gastrointestinal muscles.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 cup of water and 1 teaspoon of dried slippery elm bark.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-10 minutes.
Remove from heat, and let it steep for an additional 10 minutes.
Sip the tea slowly, a quarter of a cup at a time, after meals or as needed for heartburn relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Licorice tea helps with heartburn because it contains a compound called glycyrrhizin, which can increase the mucus lining of the esophagus and stomach, protecting them from acidic contents. This action helps prevent the backflow of stomach acid into the esophagus, reducing the likelihood of heartburn. Additionally, licorice tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe irritated tissues in the digestive tract, further alleviating discomfort. It's important to note that excessive consumption of licorice tea can lead to side effects like high blood pressure and potassium depletion, so it should be consumed in moderation. Deglycyrrhizinated licorice (DGL) tea, which has had the glycyrrhizin removed, is a safer alternative for long-term use. Lastly, while licorice tea can be beneficial for occasional heartburn, it is not a substitute for medical treatment, especially for chronic or severe heartburn symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain glycyrrhizin, which can help balance stomach acid and soothe heartburn symptoms.
Rhizomes: Licorice rhizomes have been used in traditional medicine to treat digestive issues, including heartburn, due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: The leaves of the licorice plant are rich in flavonoids, which may help relieve heartburn by protecting the stomach lining.
Flowers: Licorice flowers can help stimulate mucus production, providing a protective barrier in the stomach and esophagus and helping to alleviate heartburn symptoms.
Seeds: Licorice seeds may help reduce inflammation and irritation in the digestive tract, making them useful for managing heartburn.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: This compound provides the sweet taste in licorice and has been shown to have anti-inflammatory effects, which may help reduce irritation in the esophagus caused by heartburn.
Flavonoids: These compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help protect the esophageal lining and reduce inflammation caused by heartburn.

Glycyrrhetic acid: This compound has been shown to increase the mucus production in the gastrointestinal tract, which can help protect the esophagus from stomach acid and alleviate heartburn symptoms.
Chalcones: These compounds have been found to have gastroprotective effects, which can help prevent and treat gastric ulcers and alleviate heartburn symptoms.
remedy_recipe: In a teapot, place one licorice root tea bag or 1-2 teaspoons of loose licorice root, depending on preference.
Pour 8 ounces of freshly boiled water over the tea bag or loose licorice root.
Allow the tea to steep for 5-10 minutes to extract its beneficial compounds.
Remove the tea bag or strain out the loose licorice root, then let the tea cool slightly to a drinkable temperature.
Sip the licorice tea slowly, enjoying its soothing properties to help alleviate heartburn symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamon tea helps with heartburn because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus caused by acid reflux. The natural compounds found in cinnamon, such as cinnamaldehyde, can inhibit the growth of bacteria responsible for causing acid production in the stomach, thereby preventing heartburn. Moreover, cinnamon tea has been shown to stimulate the production of digestive enzymes, which can help speed up digestion and prevent stomach acid from backing up into the esophagus. Additionally, cinnamon tea can help soothe and calm the stomach muscles, reducing the likelihood of spasms and acid reflux. Furthermore, the antioxidant properties of cinnamon tea can help reduce the damage caused by free radicals in the digestive system, which can contribute to the development of heartburn and other digestive issues.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of cinnamon plants are often used in medicinal teas for heartburn due to their richness in antioxidants, which can help soothe the digestive tract.
Bark: Cinnamon bark is popularly used in teas for heartburn relief, as it contains compounds that can reduce inflammation and help regulate stomach acid production.
Seeds: The seeds of cinnamon plants, when used in medicinal teas, can aid in relieving heartburn symptoms, thanks to their natural oil content that can stimulate digestion.
Flowers: Cinnamon flowers are used in tea for heartburn treatment as they have anti-inflammatory and carminative properties that can help alleviate gastrointestinal discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Cinnamaldehyde: This compound is the main active ingredient in cinnamon and gives it its distinct flavor and aroma. It has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate heartburn.

Eugenol: This compound is found in smaller amounts in cinnamon, but still contributes to its medicinal properties. It has analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help relieve pain and inflammation associated with heartburn.

Tannins: These are polyphenolic compounds that are known for their astringent properties. They can help reduce inflammation and irritation in the digestive tract, which can provide relief from heartburn.
Coumarins: These compounds have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help protect the digestive tract from damage and reduce inflammation.

Methylhydroxychalcone polymer: This compound has been shown to have insulin-sensitizing effects, which can help regulate blood sugar levels. This is important for heartburn relief, as high blood sugar levels can exacerbate heartburn symptoms.
remedy_recipe: In a small pot, boil 1 cup of water for 3-5 minutes.
Stir in 1 cinnamon stick and 1 teaspoon of honey, letting it steep for 10 minutes.
Remove cinnamon stick, then add 1/2 teaspoon of apple cider vinegar.
Sip and enjoy the soothing cinnamon tea for heartburn relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginger tea helps with heartburn because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, thereby alleviating the symptoms of heartburn. Additionally, ginger is known to help speed up gastric emptying, which can prevent the buildup of stomach acid that can cause heartburn. Furthermore, ginger contains compounds called gingerols and shogaols, which can help relax the smooth muscle in the digestive tract, reducing the likelihood of acid reflux. Ginger tea is also a natural remedy for nausea, which can be a common symptom of heartburn, making it a soothing and effective treatment option. Finally, ginger tea is a caffeine-free alternative to other beverages, such as coffee and soda, which can trigger heartburn in some individuals.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: The underground stem structure of ginger, used in medicinal tea for heartburn due to its anti-inflammatory and digestive properties.
Roots: Packed with gingerols, the ginger roots are beneficial for soothing the stomach and reducing inflammation, making it ideal for heartburn relief.
Leaves: Containing essential oils and antioxidants, ginger leaves can help alleviate digestive issues like heartburn by promoting the elimination of gas and reducing inflammation.
Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and bioactive compounds, ginger flowers can help relax the gastrointestinal muscles and relieve discomfort caused by heartburn and acid reflux.
Seeds: Ginger seeds are a natural remedy for heartburn due to their carminative and analgesic properties, which can reduce stomach spasms and offer relief from symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: Gingerols are the primary anti-inflammatory compounds in ginger, which can help relieve irritation and reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, providing heartburn relief.
Shogaols: Shogaols, derived from gingerols, have been shown to exhibit gastroprotective properties, helping to prevent or alleviate gastric damage, including heartburn-related discomfort.

Paradols: Paradols, another derivative of gingerols, have been found to enhance gastric slow-wave properties, potentially aiding in the regulation of gastric motility and reducing symptoms associated with heartburn.

Zingiberene: As a major constituent of ginger, zingiberene contributes to ginger's carminative properties, helping to expel gas from the stomach and alleviate bloating, thereby reducing factors that can contribute to heartburn.
remedy_recipe: Peel and grate 1 inch of fresh ginger; let it steep in hot water for 10 minutes.
In a teapot or cup, add 1 tablespoon of honey and the juice of half a lemon to the ginger-infused water.
Stir the mixture well until the honey is completely dissolved.
Warm up to drinking temperature, if necessary, but avoid boiling to preserve beneficial enzymes.
Pour into a cup and enjoy your soothing ginger tea for heartburn relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cloves tea helps with heartburn because it has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help reduce inflammation and provide pain relief in the esophagus. The tea's natural flavorings and compounds, including eugenol, can aid in the healing process of the digestive tract lining, thus preventing acid reflux. Moreover, cloves tea can stimulate the production of digestive enzymes and gastric juices, improving digestion and preventing the occurrence of heartburn. The tea can also act as a natural antacid, balancing the stomach's pH levels, which can further prevent acid reflux and alleviate heartburn symptoms. Ultimately, drinking cloves tea can provide a soothing and calming effect on the digestive system, promoting overall digestive health and preventing heartburn.
remedy_parts: Seeds: The seeds of the cloves plant are used in medicinal tea for heartburn because they contain eugenol, a compound with anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can help reduce irritation in the digestive tract.

Buds: The buds of the cloves plant are used in medicinal tea for heartburn due to their high concentration of eugenol and flavonoids, which have been shown to have gastroprotective effects and can help reduce gastric acid production.

Leaves: The leaves of the cloves plant are used in medicinal tea for heartburn because they contain tannins, which have astringent properties that can help soothe inflammation and irritation in the esophagus and stomach.

Flowers: The flowers of the cloves plant are used in medicinal tea for heartburn due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the digestive tract.

Stems: The stems of the cloves plant are used in medicinal tea for heartburn as they contain essential oils that have been shown to have antispasmodic effects, which can help relieve symptoms of heartburn such as abdominal pain and spasms.
remedy_constituents: Eugenol: This compound found in cloves has been found to have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help reduce irritation and discomfort in the digestive tract caused by heartburn.
Acetyl Eugenol: This compound has been shown to have gastroprotective properties, meaning it can help protect the lining of the stomach and reduce the symptoms of heartburn.

Kaempferol: This flavonoid found in cloves has been found to have anti-oxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and relieve heartburn symptoms.
Oleanolic Acid: This compound found in cloves has been shown to have gastroprotective and anti-ulcer properties, which can help protect the stomach lining and reduce the symptoms of heartburn.
remedy_recipe: In a small pot, boil 1 cup of water for 3-5 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of whole cloves to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let the mixture simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Sip the cloves tea slowly, allowing it to soothe heartburn.

remedies.remedy_desc: Turmeric tea helps with heartburn because it contains a powerful compound called curcumin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, thereby alleviating heartburn symptoms. Additionally, curcumin can stimulate the production of bile, which aids in digestion and can prevent the buildup of stomach acid that leads to heartburn. Furthermore, turmeric tea has been shown to improve gut motility, allowing food to move through the digestive system more efficiently and preventing acid reflux. The antioxidant properties of turmeric tea can also protect the lining of the stomach and esophagus from damage caused by stomach acid, further reducing the risk of heartburn. Finally, turmeric tea is a natural and caffeine-free alternative to traditional heartburn remedies, making it a safe and effective long-term solution for managing heartburn symptoms.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: The underground stem of turmeric, rich in curcumin, is used for its anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe heartburn.
Roots: Turmeric roots, with similar properties as rhizomes, can aid in digestion and alleviate symptoms of heartburn.
Leaves: High in antioxidants, turmeric leaves can help reduce inflammation and provide relief for heartburn discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: The most active compound in turmeric, known for its anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce irritation in the esophagus and relieve heartburn.
Turmeric oil: Contains beneficial compounds like turmerone, that can stimulate the production of digestive enzymes, promoting healthy digestion and preventing heartburn.
Bisdemethoxycurcumin: A curcumin derivative that has strong antioxidant properties, which can protect the stomach lining from damage and reduce the risk of heartburn.
Demethoxycurcumin: Another curcumin derivative, with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate heartburn symptoms.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 2 cups of water and 1 teaspoon of ground turmeric; bring the mixture to a boil, allowing it to simmer for 10 minutes.
Stir in 1/2 teaspoon of honey and a squeeze of lemon juice, adjusting the sweetness and tartness to your preference.
Optional: add a pinch of black pepper to increase turmeric absorption.
Strain the tea into a cup, removing the turmeric residue.
Sip the tea slowly, consuming it while still warm, and repeat as needed for heartburn relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Dandelion tea helps with heartburn because it aids in digestion by promoting the production of digestive juices, including stomach acid. This increase in stomach acid can help to break down food more effectively, preventing the formation of stomach contents that can cause heartburn. Additionally, dandelion tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce inflammation in the stomach and esophagus, alleviating the symptoms of heartburn. Dandelion tea is also a natural diuretic, helping to flush excess fluids and toxins out of the body, which can further reduce the risk of heartburn. Furthermore, dandelion tea contains antioxidants that can help to protect the stomach lining from damage, reducing the risk of developing heartburn and other gastrointestinal issues. Finally, dandelion tea has been shown to stimulate the production of bile in the liver, which can help to improve overall digestive function and reduce the risk of heartburn.
remedy_parts: Roots: The dandelion roots contain compounds that can help reduce inflammation and irritation in the stomach, making it an effective ingredient for heartburn tea.
Leaves: Rich in analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, dandelion leaves can help soothe and relieve heartburn symptoms.
Flowers: Dandelion flowers have been known to aid in digestion and can help reduce the production of stomach acid, providing heartburn relief.
remedy_constituents: Taraxacin: Taraxacin, a bitter principle found in dandelion, stimulates digestion and promotes the flow of bile, which can help to prevent the formation of stomach acid and reduce heartburn symptoms.

Taraxasterol: This compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation in the stomach and esophagus, thereby alleviating the pain and discomfort associated with heartburn.
Inulin: Inulin is a type of dietary fiber found in dandelion that can help to regulate digestion, prevent acid reflux, and reduce the risk of heartburn.
Potassium: Dandelion is a good source of potassium, a mineral that can help to regulate the levels of acid in the stomach, reducing the likelihood of heartburn.
Vitamin A: Vitamin A is an antioxidant that can help to reduce inflammation in the stomach and esophagus, which can help to alleviate the symptoms of heartburn.
Vitamin B Complex: Dandelion contains vitamin B complex, which can help to regulate the digestive system, reduce stress, and prevent the symptoms of heartburn.
Vitamin C: Vitamin C is an antioxidant that can help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the stomach and esophagus, which can help to alleviate the symptoms of heartburn.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 dandelion heads and rinse them thoroughly under running water to remove dirt and bacteria (2-3 minutes).
Separate the yellow petals from the green base and leaves, as the greens can increase heartburn (5 minutes).
Boil 2 cups of water in a pot, then add the dandelion petals, reducing heat and letting it simmer for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the petals, and let it cool down to a drinkable temperature (5 minutes).
Sip the dandelion tea slowly, allowing it to coat your throat and digestive tract, and drink up to three times a day as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Milk thistle tea helps with heartburn because it contains a natural ingredient called silymarin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, thereby alleviating heartburn symptoms. Moreover, milk thistle tea can increase mucus production in the stomach, creating a protective barrier against stomach acids and preventing the backflow of acid into the esophagus. Additionally, milk thistle tea has been shown to improve digestion and absorption of nutrients, reducing the production of stomach acids and lessening the likelihood of heartburn. Furthermore, milk thistle tea may help to strengthen the lower esophageal sphincter, a muscle that acts as a barrier between the esophagus and stomach, preventing the backflow of stomach acid into the esophagus and alleviating heartburn symptoms. Lastly, milk thistle tea has been used as a natural remedy for various digestive issues, including heartburn, due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, making it a popular and effective alternative to over-the-counter medications.
remedy_parts: Seeds: The seeds of the milk thistle plant are the most commonly used part for making medicinal tea to treat heartburn. They contain silymarin, a flavonoid that aids in digestion and reduces inflammation.
Leaves: Milk thistle leaves have anti-inflammatory properties which can help soothe irritated stomach lining and alleviate heartburn symptoms.
Flowers: The flowers of the milk thistle plant are rich in antioxidants and can help protect the stomach lining, reducing the occurrence of heartburn.
Rhyzomes: Although less common, milk thistle rhyzomes can be used in teas and have been shown to improve bile production, aiding in digestion and preventing heartburn.
remedy_constituents: Silymarin: This flavonoid complex found in milk thistle has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help protect the stomach lining and reduce acid reflux, alleviating heartburn symptoms.
Silybin: A potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compound, silybin helps maintain the integrity of the gastrointestinal mucosa, preventing stomach irritation and heartburn.
Silychristin: With strong antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, silychristin aids in soothing the gastrointestinal tract and reducing symptoms of heartburn.
Silydianin: This flavonolignan from milk thistle exhibits antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to alleviate heartburn by protecting the stomach lining and reducing acid reflux.
remedy_recipe: In a cup, pour boiling water over 1 teaspoon of dried milk thistle seeds.
Allow the seeds to steep for 10-15 minutes to extract the beneficial compounds.
Strain the tea to remove the seeds, leaving only the liquid.
You may add honey or lemon to taste, but avoid milk or other dairy products.
Drink the tea while warm, up to three times a day as needed for heartburn relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Fennel tea helps with heartburn because it contains a volatile oil called anethole, which relaxes the gastrointestinal tract and may help to reduce symptoms of heartburn. This oil also has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce inflammation in the stomach and esophagus, reducing the discomfort associated with heartburn. Additionally, fennel tea has been shown to stimulate the production of digestive enzymes, helping to speed up digestion and prevent the build-up of acid in the stomach that can lead to heartburn. Furthermore, fennel tea is known to have a calming effect on the stomach, reducing spasms in the gastrointestinal tract that can exacerbate heartburn symptoms. Finally, because fennel tea is a caffeine-free alternative to other beverages, such as coffee or black tea, it is less likely to irritate the stomach and exacerbate heartburn symptoms.

Peppermint tea helps with heartburn because it contains menthol, a natural relaxant that can help soothe the gastrointestinal tract and reduce inflammation in the esophagus. This substance can also help to relax the sphincter muscle at the lower end of the esophagus, which can prevent the backflow of stomach acid that causes heartburn. Moreover, peppermint tea has antispasmodic properties that can help relieve the pain and discomfort of heartburn by reducing muscle spasms in the digestive tract. However, it's important to note that peppermint tea may not be suitable for everyone, particularly those with Gastroesophageal Reflux Disease (GERD), as it can exacerbate symptoms. Therefore, it's always best to consult with a healthcare professional before consuming peppermint tea or any other natural remedies for heartburn.

Basil tea helps with heartburn because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus caused by stomach acid backing up into it. The soothing effect of basil tea on the stomach lining can help to decrease the production of stomach acid, thereby alleviating heartburn symptoms. Moreover, basil tea is known to have antispasmodic properties, which can help relax the gastrointestinal muscles and prevent acid reflux. Additionally, basil tea contains essential oils, such as eugenol and citronellol, which have been shown to have calming effects on the digestive system, further reducing the likelihood of heartburn. Lastly, basil tea is a caffeine-free alternative to other beverages, and its consumption does not contribute to the relaxation of the lower esophageal sphincter, a common cause of heartburn.

Skullcap tea helps with heartburn because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce irritation in the esophagus, which is often exacerbated by heartburn. Additionally, skullcap tea has antispasmodic properties that can help relax the gastrointestinal muscles and reduce spasms, thereby preventing acid from backing up into the esophagus. Skullcap tea may also stimulate the production of digestive enzymes, improving digestion and reducing the likelihood of heartburn. Furthermore, skullcap tea has been shown to have calming effects, which can help alleviate stress and anxiety that can sometimes trigger heartburn. Finally, because skullcap tea is naturally caffeine-free, it is less likely to irritate the stomach and worsen heartburn symptoms.

Valerian tea helps with heartburn because it contains natural compounds that have a soothing effect on the digestive system, which can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining caused by acid reflux. Additionally, valerian tea has been shown to increase the production of mucin, a slippery secretion that coats and protects the mucous membranes of the digestive tract, further preventing irritation and damage from stomach acid. Furthermore, valerian tea has natural sedative properties that can help relieve stress and anxiety, which can contribute to heartburn symptoms by triggering the body's fight-or-flight response and increasing stomach acid production. Valerian tea is also a natural antispasmodic, which means it can help relax the smooth muscles of the esophagus and stomach, reducing the likelihood of acid reflux and easing the pain of heartburn. Taken together, these properties make valerian tea a valuable tool for managing heartburn symptoms and promoting overall digestive health.

Passionflower tea helps with heartburn because it contains a natural chemical called edulilic acid, which has been found to have antispasmodic properties that can help reduce the frequency and intensity of heartburn symptoms. These antispasmodic properties work by relaxing the smooth muscles of the esophagus, allowing the muscle to function properly and prevent stomach acid from flowing back up into the esophagus. Additionally, passionflower tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus caused by acid reflux, further alleviating heartburn symptoms. Passionflower tea also has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce stress and anxiety, known triggers of acid reflux and heartburn. Furthermore, passionflower tea is a caffeine-free alternative to traditional teas, making it a better option for those who experience heartburn after consuming caffeinated beverages.

Hawthorn tea helps with heartburn because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus, which is often irritated by stomach acid during heartburn. Additionally, hawthorn tea is known to strengthen and tone the muscles in the lower esophageal sphincter, preventing stomach acid from backing up into the esophagus. Furthermore, hawthorn tea has been shown to increase mucus production in the digestive tract, providing a protective barrier against stomach acid. The tea also acts as a natural digestive aid, helping to break down food more efficiently and reducing the production of stomach acid. Lastly, hawthorn tea is a natural antacid, neutralizing stomach acid and providing quick relief from heartburn symptoms.

Yellow dock tea helps with heartburn because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce irritation in the esophagus and stomach, thereby alleviating heartburn symptoms. The tea's bitter properties can also stimulate digestive juices and bile production, improving digestion and nutrient absorption, which can help prevent heartburn caused by poor digestion. Additionally, yellow dock tea has been shown to have antacid effects, neutralizing stomach acid and creating a protective barrier in the stomach, further preventing heartburn. Furthermore, it may help to heal any damage done to the stomach lining by excessive acid production. Lastly, yellow dock tea is a natural alternative to over-the-counter antacids, which can sometimes exacerbate heartburn symptoms when used excessively.

Burdock tea helps with heartburn because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, which are often irritated by acid reflux. Additionally, burdock tea is known to stimulate the production of digestive juices, which can improve digestion and reduce the likelihood of acid reflux. The tea is also a natural diuretic, helping to eliminate excess fluids and toxins from the body, which can contribute to heartburn. Furthermore, burdock tea contains antioxidants that can help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by acid reflux. Finally, burdock tea has been used in traditional medicine to treat gastrointestinal issues, including heartburn, making it a natural and effective remedy for those experiencing discomfort from heartburn.

Nettle tea helps with heartburn because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce irritation in the esophagus and stomach lining. It contains high levels of flavonoids and antioxidants that help balance stomach acid, relieving the discomfort caused by heartburn. Nettle tea also aids in digestion by stimulating the digestive system, which can prevent acid buildup and minimize heartburn symptoms. Furthermore, it has diuretic properties which can help eliminate excess fluids and toxins from the body, reducing the pressure on the stomach and alleviating heartburn. Lastly, nettle tea can help strengthen the mucosal lining of the stomach, providing a protective barrier against stomach acid and providing long-term relief from heartburn.
